original: The Holy Roman Empire and Poland conclude the Peace of Bautzen

Black Death Massacre: Jews (except pregnant women and children) are burnt to death on suspicion of poisoning the community during the Bubonic Plague, in Freiburg im Breisgau, Strasbourg; part of a wave of pogroms across Western Europe
Gunther of Schwarzburg chosen German anti-king
Battle at Velke Kostolany: Hungarian king Mátyás Corvinus beats Bratrici
Bell chimes invented
Duke of Albany takes captured French back to Scotland
Adrian van Goes becomes land advocate of Holland
Ippolito Aldobrandini elected Pope Clement VIII
Massive flooding in England destroys around 200 square miles of coastline and results in approximately 2,000 casualties
Spain & Netherlands sign Peace of Munster, ending the Thirty Years' War
Treaty/Truce of Andrusovo signed between Tsardom of Russia & Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth
England & Netherlands sign 2nd anti-French boundary treaty
Lifeboat 1st tested at sea, by Mr Greathead, the inventor
US Congress refuses to accept 1st petition from African American
Rep Matthew Lyon (Vt) spits in face of Rep Roger Griswold (Ct) in US House of Representatives, after an argument
US population: 5,308,483; African American population 1,002,037 (18.9%)
Scottish explorer Mungo Park leaves England seeking source of Niger River
Prussia takes possession of Hanover

The original Lower Trenton Toll Bridge, which spans the Delaware River between Morrisville, Pennsylvania and Trenton, New Jersey, is opened. A later bridge opened in 1935 is also known as the Trenton Makes - the World Takes Bridge.
British explorer Edward Bransfield aboard Williams sights Trinity Peninsula, Antarctica, claiming it for Britain
The Menai Suspension Bridge connecting the Isle of Anglesey to the northwest coast of Wales opens, considered the world's first modern suspension bridge
A fire destroys two-thirds of Mayagüez, Puerto Rico.
Yerba Buena renamed San Francisco
1st election in Washington Territory; 1,682 votes cast
Charles Hallé founds Halle Orchestra in Manchester, England
William Wells Brown published 1st Black drama, "Leap to Freedom"
USS Monitor, the US Navy's 1st ironclad warship launched
Storm flood ravages Dutch coastal provinces
French President Patrice de Mac-Mahon resigns
England team presented with ashes of a bail after Sydney Test
Harry Moses 297 not out for NSW against Victoria
Victoria beat NSW after following on (NSW all out 63 needed 76)
Bobby Abel carries his bat for 132* for England in SCG Test
Capt Lugard occupies Uganda's King Mwanga's hide out
Pneumatic hammer patented by Charles King of Detroit
US flag fired on in Rio; prompt satisfaction exacted by Adm Benham
C J Eady (Tas) 1st Australian to score twin centuries (v Vic)
SS Elbe sinks after collision in North Sea, 332 killed
Tasmania beat Victoria for 1st F-C victory in 41 years
Britain and Japan sign a treaty after months of negotiating which commits each country to supporting an independent China and Korea, although it acknowledges Japan's 'special interest' in Korea
The Imperial Library first opens to the public in Calcutta - now the National Library of India, the country's largest library
Korean National Debt Redemption Movement started by Seo Sang-dong of Daegu, attempt by the Korean public to repay debt to Japan to avoid further colonization
1st rescue of an air passenger by a ship, near Havana, Cuba
The Canadian Naval Service becomes the Royal Canadian Navy
UK House of Lords rejects Irish Home Rule Bill
German submarine attack on Le Havre
No 10 batsman F W Hyett scores century on debut, Vic v Tas
French rapist-murderer Henri-Desire Landru sentenced to death
World Law Day 1st celebrated
Great Britain clinch inaugural Olympic curling gold medal with a 46-4 win over France at the Chamonix Games
Norway’s Thorleif Haug claims first of 3 gold medals at the Chamonix Winter Olympics; wins 50k cross country in a Norwegian sweep of the medals; also 18k and Nordic combined champion
Ponsford scores second 110 of the game in Vic win over NSW
Turkish government throws out Constantine VI of Constantinople
Left wins national election in Thuringen
1st radio telephone connection between Netherlands & US

Russia launches their 1st radiosonde - a battery-powered telemetry instrument carried into the atmosphere to measure various parameters and transmit them by radio to a ground receiver- from Pavlovsk, USSR.
"Lone Ranger" begins a 21-year run on ABC radio
Clarrie Grimmetttakes 7-86 for SA in Qld 2nd inn, 13-135 for match
1st theatrical presentation sponsored by US government, NYC
Adolf Hitler's proclamation on German unified states
New owners of baseball's Boston Braves survey newspaper journalists to pick a new team nickname; known as 'Bees' in 1940 but return to 'Braves' in 1941
Adolf Hitlerthreatens Jews during his speech to the German Reichstag (Parliament)
Heavy aftershocks destroy some of Chile
Cor Jongert wins 6th Dutch 11 Cities Skating Race
Hassett's second 122 of the game for Vic can't stop a NSW win
Australian troops conquer Derna, Libya
Japanese troops land on the island Ambon, part of the Dutch East Indies (Allied forces surrender 3rd Feb)
Members of the Dutch Protestant resistance against the German occupation begin publishing underground newspaper "Trouw" (Loyal) in the Netherlands
WWII: 6 British Mosquito planes bomb Berlin, Germany in daylight
WWII: Battle of Rennell Island, in the Solomon Islands -  Japanese aerial torpedoes sink USS Chicago, a US Navy cruiser
WWII: German assault on French in Tunisia
WWII: German under officers shot down in Haarlem Netherlands
The Battle of Cisterna begins in central Italy
World War II: United States troops land on Majuro, Marshall Islands
American Rangers and Filipino resistance fighters liberate over 500 Allied POWs from Japanese at Cabanatuan
German passenger ship Wilhelm Gustloff torpedoed off Danzig by a Soviet submarine - killing about 9,000 people, the world's greatest maritime disaster
1st issue of Franklin Roosevelt dime
V Winter Olympic Games open in St Moritz, Switzerland
"Robert Montgomery Presents" dramatic anthology premieres on NBC TV
Belgium refuses to allow communists to make speeches on radio

American mathematician D.H. Lehmer and computer programmer R. M. Robinson verify 2^521-1 and 2^607-1 as Mersenne-prime numbers, using  U.S. National Bureau of Standards Western Automatic Computer (SWAC) at the Institute for Numerical Analysis at the University of California, Los Angeles
Korean War truce talks deadlock
Paul Creston's 4th Symphony premieres by the National Symphony Orchestra
Belgium ends trade agreement with USSR
Fanfani government of Italy resigns
Hallgeir Brenden of Norway wins Olympic 15k cross country gold medal at the Cortina d'Ampezzo Winter Games; his 2nd career Olympic title after taking out the 18k event in Oslo (1952)
KRMA TV channel 6 in Denver, CO (PBS) begins broadcasting
KTXS TV channel 12 in Sweetwater-Abilene, TX (ABC) begins broadcasting
Soviet 500m speed skating champion Yevgeni Grishin wins 2nd gold medal of the Cortina d'Ampezzo Winter Olympics when he dead heats teammate Yuri Mikhatlov in the 1,500m in world record 2:08.6
1st 2-way moving sidewalk goes into service in Dallas, Texas
Dore Schary's "Sunrise at Campobello" premieres in NYC
UK House of Lords passes bill allowing women to take seats
Australia 1-200 1st day 4th Test v England, Adelaide Oval
Danish ocean liner MS Hans Hedtoft sinks on maiden voyage after striking an iceberg off the coast of Greenland;, 95 die
African National Party is founded in Chad
CIA OKs Lockheed to produce a new U-2 aircraft (Oxcart)
Dutch communist trade union EVC'58 disbands
Riot curtails third days play at Port-Of-Spain WI v England
Bobby Darin is youngest performer to headline a TV special on NBC
KAET TV channel 8 in Phoenix, AZ (PBS) begins broadcasting
2 members of Flying Wallendas' high-wire act killed when their 7-person pyramid collapsed during a performance in Detroit
Laughter epidemic begins at a girls' mission school in Kashasha, Tanganyika, goes on to affect 14 schools and over 1000 people
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Military coup of Gen Nguyen Khanh in South Vietnam
Ranger 6 launched; makes perfect flight to Moon, but cameras fail
"The Name Game" by Shirley Ellis hits #3
-19°F (-28°C), Corinth, Mississippi (state record)
-27°F (-33°C), New Market, Alabama (state record)
1968 NFL Draft: Ron Yary from USC first pick by Minnesota Vikings
Bobby Goldsboro records his biggest hit, "Honey"
Viet Cong and the North Vietnamese launch the Tet offensive against South Vietnames and US forces
The Beatles perform their last live gig, a 42-minute concert on the roof of Apple Corps HQ in London, England
US/Canada ISIS 1 launched to study ionosphere
UCLA starts 88 basketball game winning streak

Bloody Sunday: 27 unarmed civilians are shot (14 are killed) by the British Army during a civil rights march in Derry, Northern Ireland; the highest death toll from a single shooting incident during 'The Troubles'
Pakistan withdraws from the Commonwealth of Nations
1973 NFL Draft: John Matuszak from University of Tampa first pick by Houston Oilers
Jury finds Watergate defendants Liddy & McCord guilty on all counts of conspiracy, burglary, and illegal wiretapping
Rock band Kiss plays their 1st show at the Coventry Club in Queens, NY
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
William E Colby, ends term as 10th director of US Central Intelligence Agency (CIA); George H. W. Bush becomes 11th director of CIA (until 1977)
Edward W. Stack replaces Paul Kerr ad president of baseball's Hall of Fame
The 8th and final part of "Roots" is the most-watched US entertainment show ever with 100 million viewers
Addie Joss & Larry MacPhail elected to Baseball Hall of Fame
Rhodesia (now Zimbabwe) agrees to new constitution
Varig 707-323C freighter, flown by the same commander as Flight 820, disappears over the Pacific Ocean 30 minutes after taking off from Tokyo.
Edward Albee's play "Lady from Dubuque" premieres in NYC
Richard Skrenta writes the first PC virus code, which is 400 lines long and disguised as an Apple boot program called "Elk Cloner".
Hilbert van Thumb becomes European skating champ
Super Bowl XVII, Rose Bowl, Pasadena, CA: Washington Redskins beat Miami Dolphins, 27-17; MVP: John Riggins, Washington, RB
American Olympic diving silver medallist Bruce Kimball, is sentenced to 17 years in prison for killing 2 teenagers in a drunk driving accident; serves 4 years
Five Pharaoh sculptures from 1470 BC found at temple of Luxor
Joel Steinberg found guilty of 1st degree manslaughter of daughter
Last day of 1st class cricket for Dav Whatmore
The American embassy in Kabul, Afghanistan closes.
Iraqi troops gain control of Al Khafji inside the Saudi Arabian border
Space Shuttle STS-42 (Discovery 15) lands
100,000 Europeans demonstrate against fascism & racism
Péter Lékó becomes the youngest chess grand master.
Belgium's TV channel 2 in Flanders goes on the air
Car bomb explodes in Algiers, killing 42 and wounding 296
Jazz guitarist Kevin Eubanks officially becomes bandleader of "The Tonight Show"
Norway's Statoil announces a newly formed consortium that will supply Norwegian natural gas to the European continent
Workers from the National Institutes of Health announce the success of clinical trials testing the first preventive treatment for sickle-cell disease.
Commander of the U.S. Fifth Fleet Scott Redd states that Iran test-fired a new anti-ship missile near the Strait of Hormuz
Future Basketball Hall of Famer Magic Johnson comes out of a 5 year retirement; helps LA Lakers to 128-118 win over Golden State at the Great Western Forum with 19 points, 8 rebounds & 10 assists
Gino Gallagher, the suspected leader of the Irish National Liberation Army, is killed while waiting in line for his unemployment benefit.
Minuteman III launches
All-Star Fla Marlin catcher Darren Daulton, retires
Howard Stern Radio Show premieres in Indianapolis, Indiana on WNAP 93.1 FM
Off the coast of Ivory Coast, Kenya Airways Flight 431 crashes into the Atlantic Ocean, killing 169.
Super Bowl XXXIV, Georgia Dome, Atlanta, GA: St. Louis Rams beat Tennessee Titans, 23-16; MVP: Kurt Warner, St. Louis, QB
English King Henry II passes the Constitutions of Clarendon, attempting to restrict power of the papal clergy in England - only Thomas Beckett objects, the beginning of their quarrel
Japanese Keichō embassy, the first to travel to Europe, headed by Hasekura Tsunenaga meets with Spanish KingPhilip IIIin Madrid
After nine months of negotiations, Scottish Presbyterians sell capturedCharles Ito English Parliament for around £100,000
Oliver Cromwell, Lord Protector of the Commonwealth of England is ritually executed after having been dead for two years
English pirateWilliam Kiddtakes his greatest prize, the Indian merchant ship Quedagh Merchant, upsetting English trade and causing a global manhunt
CaptainJames Cookreaches a record 71°10' south, 1820km from the South Pole
Honoré Gabriel Riqueti,comte de Mirabeauelected President of the French National Assembly
PopePius VI's embalmed remains are finally buried five months after his death in Valance, France
Burned US Library of Congress re-established withThomas Jefferson's 6,500 volumes
John Keatscomposes his sonnet "When I Have Fears"

"Around the World in 80 Days" byJules Verneis published in France by Pierre-Jules Hetzel
Eugene O'Neill's play "Strange Interlude" premieres in NYC
Vladimir Mayakovsky's drama "The Bathhouse (Bunya)" premieres in Leningrad
"City Lights", American silent romantic comedy film directed byCharlie Chaplin, starring himself and Virginia Cherrill, premieres at Los Angeles Theater
Clarrie Grimmett7-116 in South Africa 1st innings at Adelaide Oval

AfterPaul von HindenburgappointsAdolf Hitleras Chancellor of Germany, his former WWI colleague GeneralErich Ludendorffsends a letter to him stating "this accursed man will cast our Reich into the abyss and bring our nation to inconceivable misery"
PresidentPaul von HindenburgappointsAdolf Hitleras Reich Chancellor of Germany who forms a government withFranz von Papen
Ezra PoundmeetsBenito Mussolini, reads from a draft of "Cantos"
Benjamin Britten's song cycle "Les Illuminations" premieres at Aeolian Hall, London, England with soprano Sophie Wyss and the Boyd Neel Orchestra

Elvis Presleyrecords his cover version ofCarl Perkins' song "Blue Suede Shoes"
Home ofMartin Luther King Jr.is bombed
US Congress accepts "EisenhowerDoctrine" - US offer of aid to an Arab countries threatened with communist aggression
MLB CommissionerFord Frickannounces that players and coaches, rather than fans, will vote on selections for the All-Star Game; vote returns to the fans in 1970
Paul Hindemith's symphony "Pittsburgh" premieres
"I Fall to Pieces" single released byPatsy Cline(Billboard Song of the Year 1961)
JFKasks for an Alliance for Progress & Peace Corps, a $20 billion US Aid program for Latin America

UN General Assembly censures Portugal over Angola
Ivan Sutherlandsubmits a thesis containing his Sketchpad program, a forerunner to modern-day graphic user interfaces and computer-aided design programs

Dmitri Shostakovichcompletes his 11th string quartet

Dan Jansenskates world record 500m (35.76)

Utah'sKarl Malonebecomes 2nd player in NBA history to register 34,000 career points by scoring 18 in a 90-78 win over Chicago at the Delta Center; trails onlyKareem Abdul-Jabbar(38,387 points)
Archduke Crown PrinceRudolf of Austria, heir to the Austro-Hungarian crown, is found dead with his mistress Baroness Mary Vetsera in Mayerling in an apparent suicide pact
Gillis Grafström of Sweden wins men's figure skating gold medal at the Chamonix Winter Olympics; first to successfully defend his Summer Olympic title (1920) at the Winter Games
